rows:
Clarification on the BBX reference value
needed – C502

Updated to include Email contact Qualifier
– Group 44 : COM

Advance Shipment - Added Appendix B –
Product Features

Modified for LBBX purposes:
Added segment SG25 CNT
SG25 CNT+56 : number of mother pieces
(placed in each baby shipment, needed
for Label)
SG43 NAD+JD : new address qualifier for
Port of Entry
Additional Product Features added – APP
B
Added APP D – Loose BBX usage
Other fixes/corrections
Segment CNT – explanation part: string
“Note 6066” corrected to “Note 6069”
SG31 – TOD – added two samples of
usage in explanation part (for LBBX Baby
or Mother service code)
SG44 – COM – added qualifier “AL” for
element 3155
Previous version of MIG allowed only
TEL, EML and FAX. As relevant mapping
specification for this message already
contains as well rule for AL-->MOB, I
added this option to MIG as well.

LBBX: Addtional information that Baby
Importer must be the same as Mother
Receiver

header:
Modified and expanded Appendix D, with
updated example message
rows:
SG6/RFF – Added RFF/C506/1156
SG46/RFF – Added RFF/C506/1156
SG25/MOA (Invoice Level) – Added new
notes

Added example remarks against point 4 in
Appendix F – Return shipments

Modified Appendix F – Return shipments
to include corrected mapping of Duty Free
Import Remarks (FTX with AAU qualifier)

Corrected SG32 (Invoice level) – C506,
1153, to show that qualifier for MRN is
ABT not MRN

Added notes for low value dutiable
shipments to Singapore

Removed Appendices for Reference Data
and references to them, referring to DHL
Reference Data document instead

Updated the branching diagram to reflect
the actual data structure

Updated SG65 to include note that not
used when shipping Dangerous Goods
with DHL Express

Added notes for enabling the “Perfect
Invoice” to carry further Duty/Tax amounts
on Commercial Invoice (see SG 25 –
MOA)

Update to UNH Message Header - Note
0068 to capture the file source shipping
system platform, with corresponding
change to first example message

Correct SG 31 - LOC and SG 43 - LOC to
show how DHL Express Facility Codes
should be given

Added notes for SG 25/FTX (with CCI
qualifier) on how to populate Custom
Remarks/ Instructions for if DHL render
Invoice Image (which may then be
returned to shipper)

A service string advice segment to define the characters selected for use as
delimiters and indicators in the rest of the interchange.

header: A service segment starting and uniquely identifying the message.
rows:
A segment to indicate the beginning of the message and to transmit an
identifying number (e.g. order number), type, date and the function of the
message.
A segment indicating creation date and time of the message.

A segment to indicate a monetary value for the entire consignment, e.g.
cash on delivery or insurance.

A segment used to indicate any additional information such as business
party trader type for consignor.

A segment to specify totals of the consolidation (e.g. total number of
equipment, total number of consignments, total gross weight and/or
dimensions of the whole consolidation etc.).
A segment to identify the consignor

NAD - Name and address
• A segment with account numbers, names and addresses.
A group of segments to identify a party and related contacts and references
To specify a person within a party.

To identify a communication number of a person or department to whom com
directed
A group of segments to indicate references regarding the VAT number

RFF - References
• A segment indicating the Registration Numbers (e.g. VAT number)
of the consignor
A group of segments to indicate all transport details of the consolidation

TDT - Details of transport
• A segment to indicate information related to a certain stage of the
transport
A group of segments to indicate all data on shipment level.
A group of segments to indicate all data on shipment level.

CNI - Consignment information
• A segment to indicate consignments included in the
consolidation using the transport document/message number.
DTM - A segment to indicate date and time such as expected delivery
CNT – Control Totals within the Shipment

TSR, Transport service requirements
• A segment indicating products

MOA, Monetary amounts
• A segment to indicate a monetary value for an entire shipment,
e.g. declared customs value, cash on delivery or insurance.

FTX FTX, Free text
• A segment to specify free form or process available
supplementary information, such consignment remarks
A group of segments to specify terms of delivery

TOD, Terms of delivery
• A segment to indicate product features

Place/location identification
• A segment to identify a location related to the incoterms

A group of segments containing a references and constants which apply
or shipment.
RFF, To identify a reference used by a party

GOR, A segment to indicate applicable governmental procedures related
transport of the goods.
A group of segments to indicate documents.

Documents
• A segment to indicate accompanying documents for customs
clearance
A segment to indicate date and time related to invoice.

A group of segments to indicate names and addresses of different party
roles such as Consignee/Importer/Exporter.

NAD, Name and address
• A segment with names and addresses.

LOC, Place/location identification
• A segment to specify locations in relation to the preceding NAD
segment.

A group of segments to indicate names of contact persons for delivery of
the shipments.

CTA, Contact information
• A segment to indicate the name of a contact person.

COM, Communication contact
• A segment to indicate the type of communication contact to be
used to inform the contact person.
A group of segments to indicate references regarding the delivery

RFF, References
• A segment indicating the Reference Numbers (i.e VAT number)
of different party roles such as Consignee/Importer/Exporter.

A group of segments to specify a transport service requirement and its
associated details (such as applicable commercial agreement) related to
the party.

TSR, Transport service requirements
A segment indicating products

Freetext
A segment used to indicate any additional information such as business
party trader type.

A group of segments to describe the goods items for which transport is
undertaken.

A group of segment to specify equipment in which goods are
transported

EQD, Equipment details
• A segment to specify equipment, equipment size and type used
in the transport

EQN, Number of items
• A segment to specify number of pieces of equipment required.

MEA, Measurements
• A segment to specify loading meters of the shipment.
A group of segments to describe the goods items for which transport is undertaken.

GID - Goods item details
• A segment to identify a goods item for which transport is
undertaken.

A group of segments to specify measurements applicable to a goods
item
A segment to specify measurements, applicable to a goods item
A group of segments to specify dimensions applicable to a goods item.

DIM, Dimensions
• A segment to specify dimensions, applicable to a goods item.
A group of segments to identify references to a goods item.
RFF, To identify a goods items references used by a party.
A group of segments to specify marks and numbers of a goods item.

PCI, Package identification
• A segment to indicate the type of markings on the item.

GIN, Goods identity number
• A segment containing the bar coded identification code (e.g.
CEN License plate).
A segment group to indicate dangerous goods information.

DGS, Dangerous goods
• A segment to indicate the details of the dangerous goods within
the item.

FTX, Free text
• A segment to indicate the technical name of the dangerous
goods.

A group of segments to specify measurements applicable to the
dangerous goods within the item.

MEA, Measurements
• A segment to specify measurements applicable to the
dangerous goods within the item.
A group of segments to indicate all data on Invoice level.

CNT
Control Totals within an Invoice (i.e. Total Gross Weight, Total Net
Weight)

MOA, Monetary amounts
• A segment to indicate a monetary value for an Invoice, e.g.
declared customs value, cash on delivery or insurance.

Free text
• A segment to specify free form or process available
supplementary information (i.e. Reason for Export)
A group of segments to specify terms of delivery

TOD, Terms of delivery
Incoterm - Invoice level
A group of segments containing a references related to the Invoice

RFF - References
A segment indicating the Registration Numbers (i.e. Commercial Invoice
Reference Number, MRN)

A group of segments to identify customs and other governmental
procedures and required documents.
A segment indicating the Document Function

Free text
A segment to specify free form or process available supplementary
information, (i.e. Trading Transaction Type)
A group of segments to indicate documents.

Documents
• A segment to indicate accompanying documents for customs
clearance (i.e Invoice Number, Customs Document)
A segment to indicate date and time related to invoice.
A group of segments to describe the goods line items related Invoice

GID - Goods item details
A segment to identify a line item in an Invoice

A segment to specify a country associated with a line item (i.e. Line Item
Country of Manufacture)

A segment to specify a monetary amount associated with a line item,
such as Line Item Total Customs Value, Single Item (Article) Customs
Value, etc.

Free text
A segment to specify processable supplementary information relating to
the line item (e.i Line Item Description)
A group of segments to specify measurements applicable to a line item

A segment to specify measurements, applicable to a line item (i.e. Line
Item Gross weight, Line Item Net Weight)
A group of segments to identify references to a line item.

RFF,
A segment indicating the Line Item Reference Numbers

A group of segments to specify documents and associated date(s) and
time(s) for a line item.
A segment to specify a document prepared for a line item.
A group of segments to indicate the quantity for the line item.
A segment used to indicate the charge related to the line item.
A segment used to indicate the quantity related to the line item.
Function: To head, identify and specify a message.
Function: To indicate the type and function of a message and to transmit the identifying number.
Function: A segment to indicate date and time.
Function: A segment used to indicate any additional information such as business party trader type for consigno
Function: Used to identify different parties. Although this group may occur twice, this is limited to the NAD
segment only. The other elements and groups within group 4 are allowed only once.
Function: Specify Consignee/Receiver. The first occurrence of NAD is mandatory, to indicate the consignor.
Use qualifier CZ. The second occurrence is optional. If used, it contains the name and address of the dispatch
party, if deviating from the consignor. Element 3035 contains value PW.

A systemized, international door-to-door delivery
of document shipments within Time Definite
transit times, targeted at business-to-consumer
internet retailing

A systemized, international door-to-door delivery
of non-document shipments within Time Definite
transit times, targeted at business-to-consumer
internet retailing

Systemized export or domestic pre-18:00hrs
door-to-door delivery of non-document
shipments on the next possible business day.
Sold from Service Point outlets on cash payment
terms, with DHL branded packaging included at
eight standard weight intervals.

Systemized export or import pre-18:00hrs door-
to-door delivery of document shipments on the
next possible business day.

Systemized export, import or domestic pre-
9:00hrs door-to-door delivery of non-document
shipments on the next possible business day,
with a full money-back guarantee and proactive
delivery notification.

A systemized door-to-door pick-up of an already
consolidated shipment sent from one customs
zone to another, with a consolidated clearance at
the destination gateway and subsequent delivery
of individual shipments to different receivers on
the next possible business day.

Systemized export or import pre-18:00hrs door-
to-door delivery of document shipments on the
next possible business day.

header:
Systemized export, import or domestic pre-
12:00hrs door-to-door delivery of non-document
shipments on the next possible business day,
with a full money-back guarantee.
rows:
A packaging-based product sold with a bundled
pricing approach, with a pick-up from a Shipper’s
premises, and an international export delivery of
non-document shipments by the end of the next
possible business day.

Systemized export, import or domestic pre-
10:30hrs door-to-door delivery of non-document
shipments on the next possible business day,
with a full money-back guarantee.

Systemized export or import pre-18:00hrs door-
to-door delivery of non-document shipments on
the next possible business day.

The outbound transportation of time sensitive
supplies and medicines and the associated
inbound transportation of time sensitive
diagnostic samples during the clinical trials
phase of a new medicine (sent as non-
document).

Distribution of non-sorted business
correspondence via international postal
networks such as DPWN, with a pick-up by a
DHL Express courier.

A systemized and guaranteed door-to-door pick-
up and domestic delivery of documents and
parcels on the next possible business day by
9:00hrs, targeted at time-critical business-to-
business needs.

A systemized and guaranteed door-to-door pick-
up and domestic delivery of documents and
parcels on the next possible business day by
12:00hrs, targeted at time-critical business-to-
business needs.

header:
Systemized door-to-door delivery of documents
and parcels within the same country on the next
possible business day.
rows:
A systemized and guaranteed door-to-door pick-
up and domestic delivery of document and
parcel shipments on the next possible business
day by 10:30hrs, targeted at time-critical
business-to-business needs

Systemized export, import or domestic pre-
9:00hrs door-to-door delivery of document
shipments on the next possible business day,
with a full money-back guarantee and proactive
delivery notification.

Systemized export, import or domestic pre-
10:30hrs door-to-door delivery of document
shipments on the next possible business day,
with a full money-back guarantee.

Systemized export, import or domestic pre-
12:00hrs door-to-door delivery of document
shipments on the next possible business day,
with a full money-back guarantee.

The outbound transportation of time sensitive
supplies and medicines and the associated
inbound transportation of time sensitive
diagnostic samples during the clinical trials
phase of a new medicine (sent as documents).

Systemized export or domestic pre-18:00hrs
door-to-door delivery of document shipments on
the next possible business day. Sold from
Service Point outlets on cash payment terms,
with DHL branded packaging included at eight
standard weight intervals.

Systemized export pre-18:00hrs door-to-door
delivery of document shipments weighing up to
250g on the next possible business day, within a
DHL envelope designed and branded for the
purpose.

header:
Systemized door-to-door export or import delivery
of single or multi-piece shipments and pallets
with transit times quoted in a certain number of
days.
rows:
The same approach as for the Time Definite
solution, only with the individual shipments
delivered to Day Definite transit times. To be
developed and deployed during 2009 using the
DHL ECONOMY SELECT platform.

Systemized door-to-door export or domestic
delivery of single piece shipments weighing up
to 31.5kg for customers in certain countries
sending within Europe, with transit times quoted
in a certain number of days.

Systemized door-to-door export or import delivery
of single or multi-piece document shipments
and pallets with transit times quoted in a certain
number of days.

Systemized door-to-door export or domestic
delivery of single piece shipments weighing up
to 31.5kg for customers in certain countries
sending within Europe, with transit times quoted
in a certain number of days.

Systemized door-to-door domestic delivery of
single or multi-piece shipments and pallets
with transit times quoted in a certain number of
days.

A fully flexible service with an immediate pick-up
and an export or domestic door-to-door
dedicated delivery by way of the next
commercial or network flight out, within a
target of 24 hours.

A fully flexible service with an immediate pick-up
and an export or domestic door-to-door delivery
by way of a dedicated ground vehicle, within a
target of 24 hours.

header:
A fully flexible service with an immediate pick-up
and an export or domestic door-to-door delivery
by way of an on-board courier or chartered
aircraft for the highest level of security, within a
target of 24 hours.
rows:
Scheduled door-to-door pick-up generally before
noon and an export or domestic delivery to
limited destinations within the very same
business day. At times limited to document
shipment only.

On contractual agreement with the Shipper, pre-
printed waybills for the management of his return
second leg shipments are created via one of the
available eCom tools that offers a pre-print
functionality for Global SOP approved eCom
Labels. Data Staging will ensure that the complete
set of shipment details entered into the eCom tool
will be stored until the waybill /label is actually used
within 3 months.

On contractual agreement with the Shipper, pre-
printed waybills for the management of his return
second leg shipments are created via one of the
available eCom tools that offers a pre-print
functionality for Global SOP approved eCom
Labels. Data Staging will ensure that the complete
set of shipment details entered into the eCom tool
will be stored until the waybill /label is actually used
within 6 months.

On contractual agreement with the Shipper, pre-
printed waybills for the management of his return
second leg shipments are created via one of the
available eCom tools that offers a pre-print
functionality for Global SOP approved eCom
Labels. Data Staging will ensure that the complete
set of shipment details entered into the eCom tool
will be stored until the waybill /label is actually used
within 12 months.

On contractual agreement with the Shipper, pre-
printed waybills for the management of his return
second leg shipments are created via one of the
available eCom tools that offers a pre-print
functionality for Global SOP approved eCom
Labels. Data Staging will ensure that the complete
set of shipment details entered into the eCom tool
will be stored until the waybill /label is actually used
within 24 months.

header:
At Shipper's request, the international delivery of a
shipment/piece on a Saturday, to selected
postal/zip code areas that have the capability, in
countries where Saturday is not a normal working
day. For more information about this service and
availability, please contact your Sales
representative.
rows:
At Shipper's request, the provision at individual
shipment level, of declared value coverage above
Standard Liability, for the amount necessary to
repair or replace a shipment/piece in the event of
physical loss or damage.
Called Shipment Value Protection in the US/CA.
For more information about this service and
availability, please contact your Sales
representative.

By contractual agreement with the Shipper, the
delivery of a shipment/piece only against payment
of the goods value indicated by the Shipper on the
waybill (below a certain maximum amount).
Procedures and means of payment to the Courier
depend on local conditions and practices (cash,
bank guaranteed cheques, or credit card).
Available only with domestic products.
Only a Shipper with an account and who
contractually guarantees the amount of such
shipments can use this service.
For more information about this service and
availability, please contact your Sales
representative.

indicates loose break bulk shipment (virtual
mother)

header:
At Shipper's request, the use of a feature code to
support the payment process when the Importer of
Record is different from the shipment Receiver.

rows: Applied to both Mother shipment record and Baby shipment records

Records for Baby shipments and their corresponding Mother shipment must be
submitted together in one IFCSUM file

Shipper Account given in SG4 - NAD segment (therefore applied to all shipment
records in file) with CZ qualifier, but not in Payer Account (see point 6)

DHL Product Code likely WPX (confirm with DHL Express)
As TSR+++WPX:57:87'

Dutiable shipment, therefore must include Declared Total Customs Value As
MOA+43:x:yyy' where x is numeric value to 2 decimal places and yyy is three
letter currency code

Service Code 230 given, as TOD+6++DDU:::230'
(where DDU is Terms of Trade of shipment)

Payer Account is FOC (free of charge) account. Exact value must be confirmed
with DHL Express in the shipping country - will likely consist of 9 alpha
characters including a pre-fix of FOC
As NAD+FP+FOCXXXXXX', where FOCXXXXXX is payer account

Receiver Name contains Company Name of Importer of Record c/o DHL
Express. Receiver Contact Name and details, and Receiver address details
given for DHL facility at point of entry into destination country (confirm details
with DHL Express)

Piece records against each Baby shipment also given against Mother shipment
i.e. Piece IDs shared (therefore No. of Mother Shipment Pieces = Sum of
Shipment Pieces across all Baby Pieces)

No. of Mother shipment pieces (so sum of all corresponding Baby shipment
pieces) given against each Baby Shipment
As CNT+56:x’ where x is no. of Mother shipment pieces
Has DHL Product Code BBX, as TSR+++BBX:57:87'

Corresponding Mother Shipment AWB no. given as Shipper Reference with ACL
Qualifier
As RFF+ACL:9999999999' where 9999999999 is Mother AWB no.

Mother shipment Receiver Address and Contact Details given as Port of Entry
Address and Contact Details, in NAD segment with JD qualifier with
corresponding CTA and COM segments.

header:
Given after Consignee address (NAD+CN and corresponding LOC, CTA and
COM segments)
rows: Customer’s account given in Payer Account
Receiver Contact and Address details given for end receiver of Baby shipment
Original Export Date (of line item within shipment)

Segment Group 54 (Invoice Line Item)
RFF with ZZZ qualifier
See Page 87
e.g. RFF+ZZZ:original export date'

Original Export Tracking ID (of line item within
shipment) – i.e. consignment note number of
shipment on which item/s being returned were
originally exported

Segment Group 54 (Invoice Line Item)
RFF with ERN qualifier
See Page 87
e.g. RFF+ERN:original export tracking ID'

Original Export Carrier Name (of line item within
shipment) – i.e. carrier of shipment on which item/s
being returned were originally exported

Segment Group 54 (Invoice Line Item)
RFF with CN qualifier
See Page 87
e.g. RFF+CN:2 original outbound carrier
name'

Duty Free Import Remark – i.e. free text remark to
assist in Customs submission for Return shipment
Remarks differ by re-import / destination
country. Examples:
• US: “Goods returning to the US and were not
advanced in value or condition while outside
the US”
• UK: "British Returned Goods"
• CA: "Canadian Goods Return"
• AU: “Australian goods returned”
• NZ: “New Zealand Returned Goods”

Segment Group 25 (Invoice level)
FTX with AAU qualifier
See Page 74
e.g. FTX+AAU+++Duty free import remarks’

Segment Group 25 (Invoice level)
FTX with CEX qualifier, populate with R
(Return)
See page 74
e.g. FTX+CEX+++R'
